diagrams.title: QA vs QC: Quality Assurance vs Quality Control
diagrams.description: A comparison diagram showing the key differences between Quality Assurance (process-focused, preventive) and Quality Control (product-focused, detective). This helps learners understand that QA is about preventing defects while QC is about detecting them through testing and inspection.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental distinction between QA (process) and QC (product) in software engineering

diagrams.title: Software Testing Levels Pyramid
diagrams.description: A pyramid diagram showing the four levels of software testing: Unit Testing (base), Integration Testing, System Testing, and Acceptance Testing (top). Each layer shows the scope and who performs each testing level.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the hierarchical structure and progression of software testing levels from component-level to final user acceptance

diagrams.title: QA Process Lifecycle (PDCA Cycle)
diagrams.description: A circular diagram representing the Plan-Do-Check-Act continuous improvement cycle applied to Quality Assurance in software engineering, showing how quality activities flow continuously.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the iterative nature of quality assurance processes and the continuous improvement loop in software engineering
